title: Authenticated Crawls, Sessions, Tenant Isolation, and Privacy · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent municipal service owner supplies a different origin, markup family, dynamic boundary, accessibility need, resource budget, and injected failure and transfers authenticated crawls, sessions, tenant isolation, and privacy into a changed least-authority session and privacy policy. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of least-authority session and privacy policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, c…
objectives: Require explicit owner and account authorization, purpose, role, environment, page scope, time window, and revocation for authenticated access.
Acquire secrets through approved channels, avoid source and logs, scope them minimally, rotate exposure, and destroy temporary material.
Permit safe retrieval only, block state-changing forms and logout or destructive routes, and test same-site side effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Session Authorization
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 7117 during a hostile redirect, stream, and checkpoint drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove least-authority sessions, secrets, safe methods, isolation, minimization, and derived-data deletion; browser TypeScript remains deterministic and free of Node, network, filesystem, browser, and host effects; then change one test, mutation, package, browser binary, container, capacity, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Require explicit owner and account authorization, purpose, role, environment, page scope, time window, and revocation for authenticated access.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Session Authorization.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for require explicit owner and account authorization, purpose, role, environment, page scope, time window, and revocation for authenticated access. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Session Authorization, keep Session Authorization connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Sensitive Data Controls
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: crawler-ts-sensitive-data-controls-16vwn8" that uses case 2243 to prove least-authority sessions, secrets, safe methods, isolation, minimization, and derived-data deletion. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, resolve DNS, parse through Cheerio, launch Playwright or axe-core, access files or packages, retain credentials, leave handles, or claim native runtime, accessibility, privacy, legal, load, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility compiler API, Cheerio 1.2.0, robots-parser 3.0.1, p-limit 7.3.0, csv-stringify 6.8.1, Playwright 1.61.1, axe-core 4.12.1, original fixtures, loopback servers, emitted-runtime and handle tests, controlled DNS and browser environments, accessibility review, packages, containers, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.
